summary: Gruber analyzes Apple's Q4 2008 financial results to argue that the iPhone, just 15 months old, has already become Apple's most important product line. Using non-GAAP numbers that strip out subscription-based accounting, iPhone revenue exceeded both Mac and iPod revenue, representing 39% of Apple's total. Apple outsold RIM in units and became the world's third-largest mobile phone supplier by revenue, trailing only Nokia and Samsung. Gruber projects that holiday quarter sales could reach 13.8 million iPhones, potentially surpassing all Windows Mobile phones combined. He concludes that the iPhone will soon be undeniably Apple's primary product and platform.
key_points: Apple's subscription-based accounting for the iPhone dramatically understates its quarterly revenue — non-GAAP results show income more than double the reported GAAP figures
The iPhone generated more revenue than either the Mac or iPod last quarter, making it arguably the strongest of Apple's three business legs after just 15 months
Apple outsold RIM in units (6.9M vs 6.1M) and became the third-largest mobile phone supplier by revenue worldwide, behind only Nokia and Samsung
Holiday quarter multiplier analysis suggests Apple could sell twice as many iPhones (potentially 13.8M) in the current quarter, likely surpassing Windows Mobile unit sales for the entire year
Apple's transformation from 'the Mac company' to a three-legged business is now tilting toward iPhone dominance
money_quotes: For a long time — the entire decade of the '90s and the first few years of this decade — the story of Apple was the story of a company searching for a way to be something other than 'the Mac company'.
The iPhone, just 15 months old, is perhaps already the strongest of the company's three legs.
If this isn't stunning, I don't know what is, all due to the incredible success of the iPhone 3G.
The entire iPhone platform is only 15 months old. The cheapest model still costs $199. The room for growth in this market is unlike anything Apple has ever seen.
So the question is: Despite continuing strong iPod sales and record-breaking Mac sales, how long until the iPhone is undeniably the primary product and platform made by Apple?
The iPhone may well already be outselling all Windows Mobile phones combined.
Many analysts doubted that 10 million iPhone goal for the year; Apple might in fact sell 10 million iPhones in a single quarter.
key_insight: Apple's Q4 2008 results reveal that the iPhone, obscured by subscription accounting, has already become the company's most powerful revenue engine after just 15 months — positioning Apple for a transformation from computer maker to mobile phone powerhouse.
